_note: Conviction theme CANDIDATE WATCHLIST. As of T2 (council.enabled=true) this is the candidate set the LLM council JUDGES — it ranks inflection / structural-vs-fad / cleanest-name / bull-vs-bear and PROPOSES which to trade (it does NOT discover new themes; that's T3). With council.enabled=false the paper loop consumes these directly (the T1 hand-seeded loop). Each: name, symbol (cleanest single-name expression), direction ('bullish'=tailwind→calls, 'bearish'=rollover→puts), thesis, active. The deterministic IV/cheap-convexity gate (PREREG_THEMATIC_CONVEXITY §4) decides whether each is actually traded — a beloved, richly-priced theme is vetoed however high the council's conviction. Run offline: `python -m dramatic_options.orchestrator --demo`.

themes.name: copper_electrification
themes.thesis: EXAMPLE — replace. Electrification + grid/datacenter buildout copper demand against constrained mine supply; hypothesis is an unloved industrial tailwind whose long-dated call IV has not yet priced the structural deficit ('copper-not-rockets').

themes.name: EXAMPLE_rich_theme_delete_me
themes.thesis: EXAMPLE — illustrates the IV-gate VETO path: a crowded, richly-priced theme. The --demo synthetic gives this symbol rich IV so the cheap-convexity gate rejects it. Replace or delete.
